items.q: How many crotchets are in a semibreve?
items.why: Students confuse the relative durations. A semibreve (whole note) = 2 minims = 4 crotchets = 8 quavers. Each level halves in length.

items.correct: Two or more independent melodic lines sounding together.
items.misconception: A single melody with chord accompaniment.
items.why: Students confuse polyphony with thick texture or homophony. Polyphony specifically means INDEPENDENT MELODIES - like a fugue. Melody + chords is HOMOPHONY, not polyphony.

items.q: How does a harmonic minor scale differ from a natural minor?
items.correct: The seventh degree is raised by a semitone (creating a leading note).
items.misconception: All notes are raised by a semitone.
items.why: Students think the whole scale changes. Only the 7TH is altered to create a leading note, which strengthens cadences in minor keys - producing the characteristic augmented second between 6th and 7th.

items.q: Whose 1953 Concentus Musicus Wien revived historically informed Baroque playing?
items.why: Correct answer: Nikolaus Harnoncourt. “John Eliot Gardiner” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
It's Christopher Small. “Susan McClary” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year did Glenn Gould abandon live performance?
items.why: The real figure is 1964 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
